content.claim: A prior wave of village-school amalgamations/closures already occurred between 2004-2008 in financially-pressed rural municipalities, reducing independently-operated units by approximately half, meaning current policy operates on a residual stock of schools.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: No systematic inventory of which schools were closed versus preserved
No data on political characteristics of surviving versus closed schools
Inference that survivors are disproportionately politically-protected is not directly evidenced
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
